test_name: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
passage:
Data set A: 2, 2, 2, 5, 5, 8, 11, 11, 14, 14, 14
Data set B: 2, 5, 5, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 11, 11, 14
Data set A and data set B each have 11 values. Which of the following statements best compares the median of data set A and the median of data set B?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The median of data set A is greater than the median of data set B.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The median of data set A is less than the median of data set B.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The medians of data sets A and B are equal.

choices.label: D
choices.text: There is not enough information to compare the medians.

test_name: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
passage:
$f(x)=-9x^2 + 113x$
The function $f$ models the depth below sea level, in meters, of a certain seal $x$ minutes after the seal dives into the water. What is the best interpretation of $f(2)= 190$ in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 2 minutes after the seal dives into the water, its estimated depth is 2 meters below sea level.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The seal reaches an estimated maximum depth of 190 meters below sea level.

choices.label: C
choices.text: 2 minutes after the seal dives into the water, its estimated depth is 190 meters below sea level.

choices.label: D
choices.text: Approximately 2 minutes after diving into the water, the seal returns to sea level.

test_name: November 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 1
passage: $13x + 14y = 423$ represents this situation. Which of the following is the best interpretation of $y$ in this context?

choices.label: A

choices.label: B
choices.text: The number of short-sleeved shirts sold

choices.label: C
choices.text: The price, in dollars, of each long-sleeved shirt

choices.label: D
choices.text: The price, in dollars, of each short-sleeved shirt
